title: Triticum × neoblaringhemii
taxonomy: Triticum × neoblaringhemii is a hybrid of two wheat species, Triticum aestivum and Triticum boeoticum. It is classified as a member of the family Poaceae, which is a large group of grasses that includes many economically important crops. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics, such as its morphology and reproductive structures. Triticum × neoblaringhemii is also classified as a member of the genus Triticum, which includes many species of wheat. At the species level, Triticum × neoblaringhemii is often considered a nothospecies, a taxonomic rank used to describe hybrid species that are not yet fully stabilized.

intro: Triticum × neoblaringhemii is a hybridized wheat species, created by crossing common wheat (Triticum aestivum) with spelt wheat (Triticum spelta). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of fiber, vitamins, and minerals. The bioactive constituents of Triticum × neoblaringhemii include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
